evidence_basis: exact Ash lifecycle state and gate structure
transformations: represent lifecycle gates as a structural route graph
exclude artifact bytes and user-level observations

visible_condition.plain_language: One source-anchoring intention currently crosses several exact custody and case gates before Rooms and Routes open.

available_affordances.purpose: Inspect the declared route structure and compare burden models without changing Ash state.

route_topology.exit: Rest with the source anchored to the case
missing_inputs: mobile screen recording under a real custody hold

causal_structure.input: declared source posture and exact custody receipt
causal_structure.operator: local verification followed by Case Map binding
causal_structure.observable: root node enters chronology zero and Rooms/Routes gates open
causal_structure.authority: Ash lifecycle remains source of truth; observatory cannot mutate it

research_frame.question: Does the declared Custody Root route contain a structurally concentrated interpretive passage before interaction evidence is collected?
research_frame.hypothesis: Multiple models will identify the route as a high-load design candidate while disagreeing about the source and magnitude of burden.
observable_behavior: no result becomes a user diagnosis or redesign command
alternative_explanations: real operators may experience the route as easier than the structural model predicts
copy or layout changes may reduce observed difficulty without changing the route graph
some declared dependencies may be hidden by current implementation details
expected_failure_modes: one model becomes a crowned score
structural burden is narrated as cognitive deficiency
the observatory silently commands an Ash redesign
falsifier: all models place the route below the declared high-load threshold
interaction evidence repeatedly contradicts the structural hypothesis
a simpler route graph preserves all exact custody gates without equivalent burden
abstention_conditions: route steps or dependencies are incomplete

contradictions: a single human intention crosses several exact system gates
local-only safety coexists with high interpretive density

allowed_claims: the declared route graph is structurally dense under multiple stated models
Custody Root is a high-load design candidate requiring interaction evidence
forbidden_claims: route burden proves cognition or mastery

causal_trace: the declared Ash route steps were represented as graph nodes
dependencies and support fields were counted
four burden models evaluated the same graph
model disagreement and missing interaction evidence remained visible
missingness: adult operator interaction outcomes are not included
contradictions: cross-model high-load consensus can coexist with disagreement about burden source
unresolved_relations: whether observed operator outcomes will match the structural models

static_equivalent.summary: A static path shows six custody steps, their dependencies, support levels, obstruction seams, model overlays, and the interaction-evidence hold.

name.plain_language: The source-anchoring route gathers several exact obligations into one passage.
non_equivalence: structural burden is not user deficiency
model consensus is not causal proof

context.example: compare burden across the three synthetic proving scenes

transfer_context.shared_relation_candidate: declared route structure can be compared without profiling participants
observable_correspondence: both contexts preserve model assumptions and missing evidence
alternative_explanations: the comparison may omit station-specific recovery affordances
abstention_conditions: route graphs are not structurally comparable
